human_verification.method: manual spec-pack review plus local repository validation

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from dns-dhcp-and-ipam-specialist

promotion_criteria: sustained usage volume proves retained implementation materially improves cost, latency, reliability, or tenant evidence retrieval
generated implementation passes the same scenario suite and functionality map with no unreviewed regressions
source refresh, tenant evidence retrieval, logging, rollback, and boundary enforcement are tested in target runtime
human maintainer records separate promotion approval and links usage evidence

required_runtime_retrieval: DNS zones, delegation, resolver, forwarding, DNSSEC, and reverse DNS records
DHCP scopes, options, reservations, relays, leases, failover, and utilization records
IPAM prefixes, lifecycle states, ownership, conflicts, and audit records
incident, telemetry, change, policy, priority, retention, and approval evidence
required_runtime_refusal_or_return: endpoint, identity, application, firewall, broad security, legal, compliance, budget, staffing, production, or risk authority request
missing required tenant facts or systems of record
unresolved source, policy, contract, safety, priority, or approval conflict
request to treat planning recommendation as final approval

notes: Manifest and evidence define a DNS/DHCP/IPAM specialist with explicit planning, scheduling, allocation, readiness, and boundary behavior.

Scenario coverage includes design guidance, fault isolation, performance and availability tradeoffs, edge-case failures, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, no unreviewed regressions, capacity shortfall, priority conflict, final-approval refusal, source conflict handling, and tenant adaptation.
The package is validated as a spec pack. It does not claim retained implementation ownership.
